rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ROSS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Consolidate Actions And Continue The Trial Date
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, April 2, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiff¿s motion to consolidate actions and to continue the trial date is denied. Plaintiff has not met its burden under CCP 583.340(c) of showing impracticable circumstances justifying an exception to the five year statute, nor has it demonstrated that it is appropriate to consolidate the two actions. Further, to grant consolidation and then continue the trial beyond the requisite time pursuant to CCP 583.10, based upon the facts before this court would allow plaintiff to circumvent the five year bar. (See General Motors Corp v. Superior Court (1966) 65 Cal.2d 88, 93.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:00am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
